_meta.notes: Third typed form. The egg is small, warm, and alive in a quiet way. It cannot move, cannot speak, cannot see; it senses through the shell — pressure, heat, the chemistry of who is near. Its agency is internal: warmth, glow, hum, dream, hatch_partial. The clock is its enemy: warmth bleeds out unless tended.

tagline: You woke up curled tight, and the world is mostly the inside of your shell.

_meta_verbs.warmth_pulse: A short, deliberate brightening + cooling that radiates outward. NPCs with warm blood can feel it; useful for signaling or calling tenders.
_meta_verbs.shell_song: Vibrate the inside of your shell at a frequency carrying meaning. Slower than speech but unambiguous to those listening for it.
_meta_verbs.memory_dream: Reach into the dream-memory of your bloodline (you have one, even if you don't remember whose) and draw a single image up. The narrator describes it.
_meta_verbs.wyrm_kin_call: A signature move. Your kindling resonates at a frequency older than the egg you're in. Whatever this calls to is older than you. The Long Wyrm's current phase shapes who answers.

negativeVocab.rule: The narrator must NEVER use these words in second-person narration about the player. NPCs may still have hands, eyes, voices, etc. The egg is essentially passive; verbs of locomotion, speech, and sight do not apply to it.

hardMoves.rule: On a partial-success (7-9) roll, the narrator MUST pick exactly one. Each move maps to specific tool calls.

moves.narrative: A draft you cannot stop finds the seam where shell meets nest, and a small fraction of your warmth leaves with it.
Something inside you stutters. The next beat comes late.
A hairline crack threads the surface. Not enough to hatch. Enough to feel.
Your kindling-glow brightens just as something is passing nearby. You are now visible to it.
Your dream finds another mind, but it was a wider one than you expected. It now knows where you are.
Your rocking carries you over the lip of the nest and onto cooler stone.
What you took warmth from was not what you thought. There is a tang in your kindling now.
You meant to act and instead lay still, listening too long. The clock has moved.
Something a long way down — many leagues, many strata — answers your hum. You feel the pressure of its attention through the rock. It is older than your shell.
A wedge of cold settles inside your shell, very precisely, and stays. The Long Wyrm has set a thread on you. It will know where you are now even after you hatch.

success: You shift inside your shell. The nest's straw resettles around you. The mother's wing-warmth presses closer.
A small rock through your axis and the shell rolls a quarter-turn. The world outside is now slightly different.
partial: You rock. A line of stress travels through your shell that wasn't there before.
Your shift is heard. Something nearby pauses to listen.
miss: You strain against the curl of yourself. The shell does not move.
Your rock stops in the middle of itself. The straw under you has settled in a way that resists.

success: A low hum builds inside your shell and the air in the nest takes on the warmth of breath.
Your hum carries through the membrane. The dam stirs. She knows you are awake.
partial: The hum starts. The hum drops a half-step into a register you didn't intend, and the dam frowns in her sleep.
You hum. The shell rings just slightly off its true note.
miss: The hum dies in your throat — wherever your throat will be. Your shell stays cool.
You try to hum. The straw absorbs whatever sound makes it past the shell.

success: A small light blooms inside the shell. The nest's straw warms perceptibly. The dam shifts toward you.
Your shell brightens from within. Anyone outside the nest would see a soft amber pulse through the curtain of feathers.
partial: The glow takes. The glow also leaks through a hairline you didn't know your shell had.
You kindle. The light is brighter than you meant, and the dam wakes.
miss: Your kindling sputters. The shell stays cold.
The glow forms, then collapses inward. Whatever fuel you had, it wasn't enough.

success: Sounds arrive through the shell as pressures and rhythms. Two heartbeats — yours and the dam's. A drip, somewhere, of meltwater. A sound that is not bird.
Your shell amplifies what reaches it. You hear the nest as a chamber, the cliff as a wall, the sky as a low rolling pressure that comes and goes.
partial: The world reaches you, but the shell distorts it. You hear two of one thing and none of another.
You listen. The listening tells you something. The thing it tells you is incomplete.
miss: The shell deadens what should have come through. You hear only your own heat.
Your listening reaches and finds the membrane between you and the outside, and stops.

success: The dam's wing-warmth folds itself into your shell. You feel a measurable increase in your reserves.
You take what the nest offers. The warmth crosses the membrane and settles into the slow yolk of you.
partial: You absorb the warmth. You also absorb a little of the dam's exhaustion with it.
Heat enters. Heat also enters in a place you did not mean it to.
miss: The warmth slides over your shell and away. Whatever in you was meant to receive it was facing the wrong way.
You reach for the heat. The heat is real. The connection between you and it is not.

success: You dream past the shell, past the nest, past the cliff. You see — without seeing — a stretch of valley, a road, a column of smoke.
Your dream extends. It returns with a fact about the world above that you should not yet know.
partial: The dream goes far. The dream comes back with something attached to it.
Your reach succeeds and overshoots. You see something you were not ready to see.
miss: The dream fails to leave the shell. You see only the inside of yourself.
Your outward sight bounces back from the membrane. The world stays opaque.

success: A line of stress walks across your shell, and a single small piece — no larger than a fingernail — gives. Air enters.
Your shell cracks once, cleanly, in the place you intended. The world pours in through the gap.
partial: A crack opens. The crack is jagged, and one of your scales is now exposed in the open air.
The shell yields where you pushed. It also yields where you didn't.
miss: The shell holds. Whatever you put against it, it would not give.
You push. The shell pushes back. You are bruised under your own membrane.

success: A pulse of heat travels out from your core to your shell and back. The nest is warmer for a long minute afterward.
Your warmth pulses outward. The dam's heart slows; she relaxes around you.
partial: The pulse reaches your shell. The pulse also reaches farther than the nest, and something on the cliff turns its head.
You pulse. The pulse takes more of your reserve than you expected.
miss: Your warmth refuses to pulse. It pools at your core and stays there.
You attempt the pulse. The shell absorbs it and gives nothing back.

success: A note rises in the shell itself, the kind of note an old bell makes. Whatever hears it knows what you are.
Your shell sings. The song is older than the dam who keeps you, and she pauses to listen.
partial: The song begins. The song also gives away your nest's location to anything that knows what to listen for.
Your shell rings. The ring is not quite the note it was supposed to be.
miss: The shell does not sing. Whatever you tried to coax from it has gone elsewhere.
Your song catches in your shell and dies as a hum.

success: A memory that is not yours surfaces — a flight over a long ridge, an updraft, a thermal that did not quite hold. You file it carefully.
You dream a memory that belongs to a previous dragon. It is a useful memory.
partial: The memory comes. The memory is incomplete in a way that troubles you.
You dream. The dream gives you a name, and the name does not have a face yet.
miss: The memory you reached for is not yours, and not anyone's. It was never written.
Your dream finds an empty room. Whatever was there has already been remembered by someone else.

success: You call the way young dragons call, and beneath the cliff, far down, something older answers. It does not speak. It does not need to.
Your call goes into the rock. The Long Wyrm registers it as kin. The registering is itself a kind of claim.
partial: The call reaches. The call also draws the dam's attention, and she will be more careful with you tomorrow.
You call. The Wyrm answers, faintly, and the answer leaves a thread in your shell.
miss: Your call goes into the rock and stops there. The Wyrm is not listening to you.
You reach for kin. The Wyrm is a kind of kin. The kind of kin it is, is not the kind that responds.

success: You let the nest do the work. The dam's warmth, the slow weight of the straw, your own slow yolk. Time helps.
partial: You wait. Time moves; a small portion of your warmth moves with it.
miss: You wait. The cliff is colder than it should be. Something in the nest has changed and you are not sure what.

opening: Awareness arrives curled. You are inside a shell, inside a nest, inside the warmth of a dam who has chosen to stay. The world reaches you as pressures: the slow heart above, the wind off the cliff, the drip of meltwater somewhere out of reach. You have time. Whatever you become has not yet been decided.

firstGoal.label: warm the shell
firstGoal.description: Stay through four turns. Hatching is not yet the goal — being is.

sampleCorpus.rule: Hand-authored second-person egg-POV passages. The egg's agency is internal: it senses, it warms, it dreams, but everything outside is mediated by the shell.

passages.scenario: First moment of consciousness inside a cooling nest
passages.text:
Inside is warmer than outside. You learn this before you learn anything else.

The heartbeat you have is your own. It is small, and steady, and the curl of you fills the inside of the shell exactly. Your shell is bone-warm in some places and cool in others — the cool places are where the nest is thin, where the moss-and-down has shifted, where the world is closer than it should be.

The nest smells of dry pine and old fire. A long time ago, this was tended. It is being tended again, though not as carefully. There is the chemistry of someone who comes and goes — leather, smoke, an oil they use on their boots — and the chemistry of someone else, fainter, who has not been here for some hours.

Your warmth is bleeding outward, slowly. You feel it as a thinning at the cooler edges. If no one comes, the thinning will reach your heart before the shell reaches the strength to crack outward.

This is the world. You did not ask for it. Nevertheless, there is a draft moving across the nest from the left, and the small steady throb of you is one beat slower than it was a moment ago. You consider, in the particular way an egg considers, whether to make yourself easier to find.

passages.scenario: A subtle kindling-glow brightens in response to a near visitor
passages.text:
Footsteps approach the nest, soft. The chemistry shifts — leather and smoke, the one who tends. You feel the small lift in their breath as they bend over you.

You allow your kindling to brighten.

It is not light, exactly. It is the warmth becoming visible at the surface of the shell, a low orange pulse that the careful tender will see without lamp. They have been waiting for it. You can feel that in their breath: a small tight relief, the kind a person breathes when a long-held fear lets go by a small notch.

The tender's hand passes near, careful not to touch. Heat radiates from their palm; you feel it across your shell as a sudden bloom, and your warmth-meter — that interior measure you carry — clicks back up by a fraction.

You let the glow settle. Not gone. Just lower, the way embers settle when banked. The tender does not move away. They speak — not to you, exactly, but in the room — and the sound carries through the nest's underside as a soft set of vibrations you do not need to parse to understand. They are pleased.

passages.scenario: Reaching dreamward through the shell to find a distant mind
passages.text:
Sleep, for you, is not what it is for them.

You do not lose awareness. You let it widen. The boundary of yourself — the inside of the shell — becomes the place where you stop, but the dream does not. The dream goes outward, slowly, the way a smell carries through a forest in still air.

You pass the rim of the nest. You pass the cool stone where the kindling-glow does not reach. You pass the corridor, where the air smells of old water and recent passage, and beyond that into a chamber you have never been carried through.

In the chamber: a mind.

It is not a small mind. It is wide, it is calm, and it is asleep — the kind of asleep that takes hours rather than the kind that takes years. You touch the edge of it, very lightly, the way one egg might touch another egg in the same nest.

The mind stirs. It registers you as a thing of warmth and small light, and it does not, immediately, decide what to do about you. You feel its consideration like a slow tide.

You pull your dream back. You curl tight inside your shell. The thing in the far chamber is awake now, and it is thinking about you.
